content.problemStatement: Write a program to declare and initialize an array of integers and calculate the **multiplication** of the 1st and 3rd elements of the array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. No further data is needed for each test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the product of the first and third elements of the predefined array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case, so the program prints the product 10*25 = 250 once.
Three test cases require three identical lines, each containing the constant product 250.
For five test cases the constant result is printed five times.

solution.approach: The array is fixed as {10, 12, 25, 9, 20}. Its first element is 10 and its third element is 25, so the product is always 250. For each test case we simply print this constant value. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
